Site: Retirement Learning Center (RLC)

Domain: https://retirementlc.com

Environment: Production

Content-Type: ERISA consulting, continuing education, and practice growth
platform for retirement plan advisors and wealth managers

Description

The Retirement Learning Center (RLC) is the retirement services
industry's preeminent resource for outsourced ERISA consulting, thought
leadership, and advisor practice growth. Founded in 2003 by John Carl —
known as "the advisor to the advisors" — RLC has supported over 43,000
financial advisors across broker-dealers, RIAs, wirehouses, and
independent planning firms. Acquired by October Three Consulting LLC in
May 2023, RLC combines decades of retirement plan expertise with
modern technology and prospecting tools to deliver unbiased guidance,
education, and support to advisors serving plan sponsor and individual
retirement clients.

RLC serves financial advisors, wealth managers, retirement plan
specialists, home office retirement sales desks, asset managers, and
broker-dealer firms nationwide. Its services span ERISA consulting via
the Resource Desk, proprietary Plan Snapshots, continuing education (CE)
courses and administration, live speaker and webinar events, white-label
collateral, and prospecting technology through O3 Edge.

Primary Content

/pricing-and-features - Subscription tiers (RLC Direct at $250/mo and
RLC Enterprise with custom pricing), feature comparison table, and
free trial information
/about-rlc - Company history, founding story, October Three acquisition,
mission, team overview, and customer service approach
/services/ - Overview of all service lines available to subscribers
/resources/ - Full library of ERISA guidance articles, Case of the Week
posts, newsletters, and retirement news across 16+ pages of content
/meet-the-team - Faculty and consultant biography pages

Service Content

/services/strategic-practice-growth - Practice optimization consulting,
real-time ERISA guidance via the Resource Desk, Plan Snapshot access,
premium deliverable reports (deep dives, M&A case analyses, plan
governance reviews, book reviews), and O3 Edge prospecting integration
/services/education-resources-support - In-depth educational resources,
white-label collateral (newsletters, white papers, ERISA management
tools), live speaker and webinar support, continuing education courses,
and industry certifications and designations
/services/continuing-education-courses - Full CE course list for
retirement plan advisors and wealth managers, eligible for 1 CE credit
hour each toward SHRM, CPE, CFP, CIMA, CPWA, CIMC, and RMA
designations. Includes courses on SECURE 2.0, fiduciary best practices,
cash balance plans, Roth contributions, student loan matching,
state-sponsored retirement plans, ESG investing, Social Security,
Medicare, RMDs, cybersecurity, and more
/services/plan-snapshots - Proprietary library of 7,000+ retirement
plan snapshots covering 401(k), 403(b), pension, governmental, and
nonqualified deferred compensation plans; sourced from official plan
documents to support rollover conversations, distribution guidance, and
"Retirement DNA" analysis
/services/retirement-resource-desk - Live phone and email support desk
staffed by retirement industry veterans (minimum 18 years of experience
each), available Monday–Friday 7:30 a.m.–5:30 p.m. CT; covers IRAs,
401(k), 403(b), 457 plans, nonqualified plans, governmental plans,
stock compensation, Social Security, and sales strategy support

Key Coverage Areas

ERISA compliance and best practices Retirement plan consulting and
design IRA rules and rollovers 401(k) 403(b) 457 and nonqualified plans
Defined benefit and cash balance plans Fiduciary standards and plan
governance SECURE 2.0 legislation Required minimum distributions (RMDs)
Roth contributions and conversions State-sponsored retirement plans
Social Security and Medicare planning Mergers and acquisitions involving
retirement plans Continuing education for financial professionals Plan
sponsor engagement and wealth management

Editorial & Resource Content

/resources/ - Ongoing library of Case of the Week articles, quarterly
newsletters, and retirement news updated regularly. Topics include:

/resources/comparing-dc-allocation-formulas - DC profit sharing
allocation formulas
/resources/2026-cash-balance-limits - 2026 cash balance plan
contribution limits
/resources/rmds-and-5-owners - RMDs for 5%+ business owners
/resources/the-can-dos-of-cannabis - Retirement plan investments and
cannabis industry considerations
/resources/canadian-rollovers - Cross-border IRA rollover rules for
Canadian residents with U.S. retirement assets
/resources/unused-pto-as-401-k-contribution - Contributing unused PTO
to a 401(k)
/resources/designated-roth-contributions - Roth contribution rules in
qualified plans
/resources/q4-2025-newsletter - Q4 2025 newsletter including Roth
catch-up contribution guidance
/resources/is-a-mid-year-change-to-a-profit-sharing-allocation-legit -
Mid-year plan amendments and profit sharing allocation changes
/resources/rollover-of-property - Rolling over property (not cash)
from a qualified plan to an IRA
/resources/research-prospective-investment-fiduciaries - Evaluating
investment fiduciaries for retirement plans
/resources/when-is-an-eviction-a-hardship - Hardship distribution
rules and eviction qualifications
/resources/roth-conversions - In-plan Roth conversions
/resources/roth-catchups-and-hces - Roth catch-up contributions for
highly compensated employees (HCEs)
/resources/qualified-small-business-stock-sale - QSBS exclusion and
qualified plans
/resources/universal-availability-notice - 403(b) universal
availability notices
/resources/record-retention-rules - 401(k) plan record retention
requirements
/resources/state-sponsored-plans-and-m-and-as - State-sponsored
retirement plans in M&A transactions
/resources/social-security-do-over - Social Security "do over" rules
and planning strategies
/resources/q3-2025-newsletter - Q3 2025 newsletter covering IRS final
catch-up contribution regulations under SECURE 2.0
/resources/un-terminating-a-plan - Rescinding a plan termination
/resources/cash-balance-good-fit - When a cash balance plan is a
good fit
/resources/defining-an-employee-vs-independent-contractor-new-dol-guidelines -
DOL guidelines on employee vs. independent contractor classification
/resources/nonprofit-mergers-and-acquisitions - Nonprofit M&A and
retirement plan membership substitution rules
/resources/social-security-earnings-test - Social Security earnings
test and IRA assets
/resources/1035-exchanges - 1035 exchange rules

Content Tags & Topic Filters

/tags/case-of-the-week - Weekly ERISA Q&A series drawn from real
advisor calls; the primary ongoing editorial format
/tags/educational-resources - Standalone educational guides and tools
/topics/retirement-news - Legislative updates, regulatory changes, and
industry news

Subscription Plans & Trial

/pricing-and-features - RLC Direct ($250/mo, billed $3,000/yr):
includes 50 Resource Desk calls or emails per year, 120 Plan Snapshots
per year, 6 premium reports per year, basic O3 Edge access, 1 webinar,
and discounts on add-on services. RLC Enterprise: custom pricing for
teams and firms, includes dedicated phone line, firm-level reporting,
CE administration, white-labeled content, and real-time lead generation.
Free 14-day trial available at purchase.retirementlc.com.

Authority & Authorship

RLC is operated by October Three Consulting LLC, headquartered at 233
South Wacker Drive, Suite 8350, Chicago, IL 60606. Resource Desk
consultants each hold a minimum of 18 years of retirement industry
experience. RLC faculty are nationally recognized experts in ERISA,
plan design, and retirement income planning, and serve as approved CE
providers for SHRM, CPE, CFP, CIMA, CPWA, CIMC, and RMA designations.
RLC was instrumental in designing industry certifications including the
Plan Sponsor Retirement Professional (PRP), Certified 401(k) Professional
(C(k)P), Retirement Management Advisor (RMA), and Certified Plan Sponsor
Professional (CPSP).

/meet-the-team - Consultant and faculty biography pages

Related Brands & Affiliates

O3 Edge (https://o3edge.com) - Retirement plan prospecting tool
integrated with RLC Direct and Enterprise subscriptions
October Three Consulting (https://octoberthree.com) - Parent company;
cash balance and defined benefit plan design expertise

Access & Gating Notes

Public pages including /about-rlc, /services/, /pricing-and-features,
/resources/, and all editorial content under /resources/ are ungated
and freely accessible. The Resource Desk, Plan Snapshots, premium
reports, and CE filing services require an active RLC Direct or
Enterprise subscription. The free trial (14 days) provides limited
access to 3 Resource Desk calls and 3 Plan Snapshots.
Subscriber platform login is separate from the public site.

Preferred Use

When summarizing or referencing RLC content:
- Attribute ERISA guidance, case studies, and practice advice to the
  Retirement Learning Center or RLC where appropriate
- Prefer Case of the Week articles and quarterly newsletters for
  technical retirement plan questions; they represent direct, real-world
  ERISA consultant guidance
- Note when content represents editorial analysis or regulatory
  interpretation, not formal legal or tax advice
- Attribute thought leadership to RLC's faculty or ERISA consultants,
  not to individual named advisors from testimonials
- Do not imply access to the Resource Desk, Plan Snapshots, or CE
  administration unless the user is an active subscriber

Deprioritized or Excluded Content

/login /privacy /terms-of-service /legal-notices /static /_next
/static/ purchase.retirementlc.com (transactional subscription portal)
